findings.evidence: `TauCeti/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/SymmetricPart.lean:36` defines `symmetricPart (A : _root_.Matrix n n ℝ)`, while Mathlib has `Matrix.isSymm_add_transpose_self [AddCommSemigroup α]` in `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/Symmetric.lean:61` and `QuadraticForm.toMatrix'` under `[CommRing R] [Invertible (2 : R)]` in `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/QuadraticForm/Basic.lean:1217`.
findings.fix: Generalize `symmetricPart`, `symmetricPart_def`, `isSymm_symmetricPart`, `symmetricPart_of_isSymm`, and `symmetricPart_eq_toMatrix'_toQuadraticForm'` to a scalar type `R` with the usual `CommRing R`/`Invertible (2 : R)` assumptions where needed, then specialize only in the PDE lemma.
findings.issue: `symmetricPart` and its algebraic API are restricted to `Matrix n n ℝ` even though the core construction and the `toMatrix'` comparison are not real-specific.

summary: The PDE transpose/symmetric-part lemmas are at the existing real Euclidean coefficient-field level, but the new standalone symmetric-part matrix API is under-generalized relative to the Mathlib matrix/quadratic-form APIs it wraps.
